evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot correctly encodes log2FC on x, -log10(p) on y, classifies 235 up / 230 down / 2535 ns with threshold lines and top-5 annotations — fully matches brief.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' both set; font stack leads with Arial/Helvetica before DejaVu Sans fallback; 9pt base with sensible 8/7.5/7pt hierarchy.
evidence.palette: Red (#C0392B) up / blue (#2471A3) down / grey (#AAAAAA) NS — directional discipline correct, red-blue distinguishable under deuteranopia/protanopia, no green/red confusion.
evidence.layout_density: 6×5 in figure, tight_layout with rect=[0,0.05,1,1] reserves space for below-axis legend; s=5/7 scatter avoids overplotting 3000 points; no gaping whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption counts match script output and thresholds are explicit; legend placed below in 3-column row is clean; gene labels are synthetic indices (G{i}) rather than real symbols — minor but visible.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is 154 KB (non-trivial); pdf.fonttype=42 ensures Type-42 embedded fonts; scatter rasterized=True is correct practice for dense point clouds in PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (154 KB), figure.png (221 KB), caption.tex (553 B), gen_figure.py (4.4 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states axes, threshold values (|log2FC|=1, p=0.01), exact counts for all three classes, and notes top-5 labelling — specific numerical claim present.

overall_strengths: Fully publication-ready typography stack: pdf.fonttype=42, svg.fonttype=none, Arial-first font family, and a clean size hierarchy throughout.
Caption is precise and self-contained, with exact gene counts and threshold values that match the script output.
overall_weaknesses: Gene annotations use array indices (G0, G1, …) rather than meaningful identifiers; a reviewer would immediately ask for real gene symbols.
Red-blue palette, while functional, is not maximally colorblind-safe for tritanopia; adding a shape or size cue for up/down would make the figure fully accessible.
